title: trichophyllum ochreatum
intro: Trichophyllum ochreatum, also known as the ochre-bristle club moss, is a species of plant in the genus Trichophyllum, which belongs to the family Polytrichaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Trichophyllum ochreatum include its ability to soothe sore throats and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested and dried to be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
